manualForm.errorOutOfBounds: Coordinates must be within Malaysia (lat 0.85–7.4, lng 99.6–119.3)

manualModal.title: Enter Coordinates Manually
manualModal.description: Type a latitude and longitude inside Malaysia to centre the map there.

processing.title: Analyzing your rooftop...
processing.description: Fetching satellite data and solar potential. This usually takes 15–30 seconds.

error.noImagery: No solar imagery is available for this location. Try a nearby address.
error.outOfBounds: Please pick a location inside Malaysia.
error.geocodeFailed: Couldn't read that location. Try clicking again.
error.rooftopAnalysisFailed: Solar data analysis failed for this location. Please try a different building.
error.checkStatusFailed: Failed to check rooftop analysis status
error.timeout: Rooftop analysis is taking longer than expected. The Google Solar API may be slow or this location may not have sufficient data. Please try again or try a different address.

consentModal.title: Lower-Resolution Imagery
consentModal.description: High-quality satellite data is not available for this address. We can proceed with BASE-tier imagery and expanded coverage instead, but accuracy will be lower.
consentModal.bullet1: Suggested panel positions may need more manual adjustment.
consentModal.bullet2: Flux and irradiance estimates are at a coarser resolution.
consentModal.bullet3: Coverage may extend slightly beyond the building outline.

coverageModal.title: Where Solar Coverage Works Best
coverageModal.description: Google's high-resolution satellite imagery is only available in select Malaysian regions. Picking a building inside one of these areas gives the most accurate analysis.

regions.name: Klang Valley
regions.cities: Klang, Port Klang, Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, Puchong

regions.name: Ipoh Region
regions.cities: Ipoh, Tambun, Lahat, Menglembu, Tanjung Rambutan

coverageModal.baseDescription: Buildings outside these regions fall back to lower-resolution data. You can still proceed, but suggested panel layouts and energy estimates will be less precise.
coverageModal.buildingTypeDescription: The Solar API was trained on individual rooftops, so it works best on residential buildings — terrace, semi-detached, and bungalow houses. Apartments, condos, shopping malls, and other large or shared-roof buildings often return inaccurate or unusable layouts.

tour.step1Body: This tool helps you estimate how much you could save on your electricity bill by installing solar panels. Let's start by finding your home.
tour.step2Body: Type your full home address or postcode here. The map will zoom to your building and show its outline.
tour.step3Body: After selecting an address, you'll see your building highlighted. Click "Analyze This Location" to fetch satellite solar data. This usually takes 15–30 seconds.
